reviews.reason: ⚡ marks the energy topic already explicit in the front and gives no clue about whether a system is one object, several objects, or an energy-storage change.
🔀 represents the multiple processes/system changes already stated in the front without identifying heating, force-work, current-flow work, or distinguishing any choice.
🏃 marks the explicitly named kinetic-energy and speed context but reveals neither the factor ½ nor which variable is squared.
🔗 is a conventional elastic/linkage landmark for the spring-and-extension context already explicit in the front; it gives no information about the factor ½ or squared variable.
🌌 marks the explicitly named gravitational context without revealing multiplication, division, a squared term, or a factor ½.
🌡️ represents the temperature-change context already explicit in the front and does not reveal the variables' relationship or distinguish any equation choice.

📘 is the correct neutral fallback. The strongest topical alternative, 🌡️, would introduce temperature—the hidden definition content absent from the front—even though temperature appears across the choices; energy-themed alternatives would likewise introduce required-energy content.
⚙️ serves as a generic practical-activity landmark for the context already explicit in the front and does not identify specific heat capacity or favor any listed measured or linked quantity.
